findings.evidence: `rg` shows the definitions and generic API in `Chebyshev/Measure.lean:167`, `:216`, and `:222`, while the new file imports only that module and adds the specializations at `Acceptance.lean:28`, `:35`, `:42`, and `:50`.
findings.fix: Move these four lemmas into `TauCeti/Analysis/SpecialFunctions/Trigonometric/Chebyshev/Measure.lean` near `normalizedChebyshevT` and `coeFn_normalizedChebyshevTLp`, then delete the extra file unless it contains genuinely downstream acceptance-only material.
findings.issue: `normalizedChebyshevT_zero_apply`, `normalizedChebyshevT_one_apply`, `coeFn_normalizedChebyshevTLp_zero`, and `coeFn_normalizedChebyshevTLp_one` are split into a new `Acceptance.lean` file even though they only specialize `normalizedChebyshevT`/`normalizedChebyshevTLp` from `Measure.lean`.

summary: The new low-degree normalized Chebyshev facts are in the wrong home: they are direct API for definitions already introduced in `Chebyshev/Measure.lean`, not a separate acceptance layer.
